rationale.R1.1: The section opens with a contribution claim ('unconditionally A-stable', adaptive partitioning) and a gap statement (fixed-splitting IMEX limitations); verifying these are coherent and justified is expected in any review of this material.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the section contains a direct logical contradiction — the abstract asserts 'unconditionally A-stable across the full range of stiffness ratios' yet the stability analysis proves |R(z)| ≤ 1 only when h|λ| ≤ 2, which is a step-size restriction, not unconditional stability. The section then confirms the scheme reverts to ERK behavior beyond that regime. Detecting and flagging such internal inconsistencies is the highest-stakes responsibility in reviewing a technical mathematics section.
rationale.R1.3: The section asserts 4–7x speedup over ARK4 and stability preservation across three named benchmarks; whether these performance and accuracy claims are adequately supported by reported experiments or tables is a central review concern.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is a standard expected output of 'review this section' regardless of technical depth; sentence-level and paragraph-level quality falls within scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on mathematical and technical correctness (validity of stability proofs, order-of-accuracy claims, internal logical consistency), or primarily on writing quality and presentation (clarity, sentence structure, narrative flow)?
